item.title: 日本官员片山表示，外汇走势与基本面不符
item.description: 日本财务大臣表示，近期汇率的波动与基本面不符，并重申当局可能采取行动加以应对。【原文：Japan’s finance minister said recent currency moves are not in line with fundamentals, reiterating warnings of possible action by authorities to counter the moves.】

item.title: 中国铝业吸引因战争而转移的原材料
item.description: 中国铝生产商有望从中东地区原材料供应路线的调整中受益。【原文：Chinese aluminum producers are set to benefit from a rerouting of raw materials away from the Middle East.】

item.title: 英国将投资10亿英镑用于量子计算研究与试验
item.description: 未来四年，英国将在量子计算研究领域投入超过10亿英镑（13亿美元），各国政府正加大对这项技术的投资力度，因为该技术被越来越视为国家安全的关键所在。【原文：The UK will spend more than £1 billion ($1.3 billion) on quantum computing research over the next four years, as governments boost investment into a technology that’s increasingly considered critical to national security.】

item.title: 随着伊朗战争引发亚洲资产市场震荡，全球基金将目光投向马来西亚
item.description: 早在伊朗战争引发能源价格飙升之前，马来西亚就已从东南亚各国中脱颖而出，成为全球投资者眼中的新宠。【原文：Even before the war in Iran sparked a surge in energy prices, Malaysia stood out from its Southeast Asian peers as the newfound darling of global investors.】

item.title: 摩根士丹利称，私人信贷违约率将升至8%
item.description: 摩根士丹利表示，随着人工智能的进步继续颠覆软件行业，直接贷款的违约率将攀升至8%。【原文：Default rates in direct lending will climb to 8% as advances in artificial intelligence continue to disrupt the software industry, according to Morgan Stanley.】

item.title: 美元走软，交易员权衡石油供应短缺问题，金价持稳
item.description: 受美元走软以及交易员权衡各方为缓解中东战争引发的石油供应冲击所作努力的影响，金价基本持平。【原文：Gold was little changed, after the US dollar slipped and traders weighed attempts to contain an oil-supply shock arising from the war in the Middle East.】

item.title: 里夫斯在英国增长愿景中承诺实现七国集团中最快的AI应用
item.description: 蕾切尔·里夫斯承诺，英国将比其他任何七国集团国家更快地采用人工智能技术，她称这项技术是其推动经济增长计划的关键支柱。【原文：Rachel Reeves vowed the UK will adopt artificial intelligence faster than any other Group of Seven nation, as she hailed the technology as a key plank of her bid to spur economic growth.】

item.title: 油价回落提振市场情绪，亚股料将上涨：市场综述
item.description: 随着油价回落提振华尔街股市，加之市场寄望于将有更多油轮能够通过霍尔木兹海峡，亚洲股市有望上涨。【原文：Stocks in Asia were poised to rise after a pullback in oil prices lifted Wall Street, amid hopes that more tankers will be able to pass through the Strait of Hormuz.】

item.title: 油价反弹，投资者正评估伊朗战争对供应的影响
item.description: 周一油价下跌逾5%后，周二出现反弹，投资者一方面评估中东地区供应面临的威胁，另一方面关注原油供应可能增加的迹象。【原文：Oil rose after slipping more than 5% on Monday, as investors assessed threats to supplies in the Middle East against signs that more crude may become available.】

item.title: 印尼料将维持利率不变，因战争及财政风险令印尼盾承压
item.description: 鉴于中东战事以及投资者对财政纪律的担忧给印尼盾带来日益增大的压力，印尼央行预计将维持基准利率不变。【原文：Indonesia’s central bank is expected to keep its key interest rate unchanged as policymakers confront mounting pressure on the rupiah from war in the Middle East and investor concerns over fiscal discipline.】

item.title: 尽管油价飙升，摩根士丹利仍维持6月降息的预测
item.description: 尽管油价飙升促使交易员们收敛了对政策制定者今年将降息幅度的押注，摩根士丹利仍维持其预测，认为美联储将于6月重启降息，并在9月再次降息。【原文：Morgan Stanley is sticking with a forecast that sees the Federal Reserve resuming interest rates cuts in June and delivering another reduction in September, even as soaring oil prices prompt traders to curb bets for how much policymakers will lower borrowing costs this year.】

item.title: 华尔街的“税收阿尔法”交易为富有的投资者节省了数十亿美元
item.description:
主动型基金经理正迎来新一轮“淘金热”，争相制定策略，帮助美国富豪大幅降低税负。【原文：

Active managers are in a new gold rush, racing to develop strategies that can help wealthy Americans crush their tax bills.

】

item.title: 石脑油短缺或将导致日本供应链陷入混乱
item.description: 尽管关于原油供应短缺的报道层出不穷，但日本工业供应链正面临着来自一种鲜为人知的石油副产品的更直接冲击。【原文：For all the headlines about the looming shortage in crude oil, Japan faces a more immediate hit to its industrial supply chain from a little-known petroleum byproduct.】

item.title: 亚洲银行家加紧努力，以安抚私人信贷市场的担忧
item.description: 随着赎回压力在规模达1.8万亿美元的私募信贷市场中蔓延，亚洲各地的私人银行家正争相安抚客户的焦虑情绪，即便是在这一被认为对近期动荡影响较小的地区也是如此。【原文：Private bankers across Asia are scrambling to contain client anxiety as redemption pressures ripple through the $1.8 trillion private credit market, even in a region seen as more insulated from the recent turmoil.】

item.title: 班克曼-弗里德的母亲被要求不要为儿子致电法院
item.description: FTX联合创始人萨姆·班克曼-弗里德的母亲曾是斯坦福大学的法学教授。但将她儿子判处25年监禁的法官表示，她不能代表儿子申请重审。【原文：FTX co-founder Sam Bankman-Fried’s mother may have been a law professor at Stanford University. But the judge who sentenced her son to 25 years in prison said she can’t act on his behalf in his bid for a new trial.】

item.title: 汇丰银行称，欧洲将面临与战争相关的天然气高价直至2027年
item.description: 据汇丰控股有限公司的一份报告显示，由于伊朗战争及霍尔木兹海峡封锁引发供应短缺，2026年欧洲天然气价格将比此前预测高出整整40%，且高企态势将持续至2027年。【原文：European natural gas prices will be a whopping 40% higher than previously projected for 2026 and will stay elevated through 2027 as the Iran war and closure of the Strait of Hormuz set off a supply shortfall, according to a report from HSBC Holdings PLC.】

item.title: 为何伊朗战争重燃了美国对滞胀的担忧
item.description: 伊朗战争在能源和金融市场引发了连锁反应，重新点燃了通胀担忧，并给经济增长带来了风险。与去年不同的是，当时的担忧源于唐纳德·特朗普总统实施的关税政策，而如今的担忧则源于人们担心油价的急剧上涨可能会成为常态。【原文：The Iran war has reverberated through energy and financial markets, rekindling inflation concerns and risks to economic growth. Unlike last year, when the source of these worries was President Donald Trump’s tariffs, the concerns now are based on fears that sharply higher oil prices could become entrenched.】

item.title: 政治界似乎尚未做好迎接人工智能的准备
item.description: 我们这个时代的决定性问题？【原文：The defining issue of our time?】

item.title: 柴油价格上涨给大豆生产大国巴西带来严重冲击
item.description: 中东战争引发的油价上涨正推高巴西农产品出口商的成本，该国种植户在运输方面高度依赖柴油卡车。【原文：Rising oil prices caused by war in the Middle East are driving up costs for crop exporters in Brazil, where growers rely heavily on diesel-powered trucks for transportation.】

item.title: 英伟达预计到2027年将通过AI芯片赚取1万亿美元
item.description: 作为人工智能计算领域迅猛扩张的核心企业，英伟达公司预计，截至2027年底，其Blackwell和Rubin芯片将带来至少1万亿美元的收入。【原文：Nvidia Corp., the company at the center of an explosive build-out of AI computing, expects to generate at least $1 trillion from its Blackwell and Rubin chips through the end of 2027.】

item.title: 布伦特原油连续第三个交易日收于每桶100美元上方
item.description: 布伦特原油连续第三个交易日收于100美元上方，创下自2022年8月以来的最长连涨纪录。投资者一方面关注短期供应充裕的迹象，另一方面则担忧中东地区能源基础设施面临的军事威胁日益加剧。【原文：Brent oil settled above $100 for a third straight session, the longest such streak since August 2022, as investors weighed signs of ample near-term supplies against rising military threats to energy infrastructure across the Middle East.】

item.title: 船只遭袭后，穿越霍尔木兹海峡的航运保险费用飙升
item.description: 据市场相关人士称，尽管成本极高，但为穿越霍尔木兹海峡的船舶投保仍是可行的。【原文：Insuring vessels to sail through the Strait of Hormuz is still possible, even if at a very high cost, according to people involved in the market.】

item.title: 美国计划在阿拉斯加建设自2013年以来首个新建燃煤电厂
item.description: Terra Energy Center正向阿拉斯加一项拟建的煤炭项目投入10亿美元，这标志着十多年来美国首次对新建燃煤发电项目进行投资。【原文：Terra Energy Center is pouring $1 billion into a deal for a planned coal project in Alaska, marking the first investment in new US coal power in more than a decade.】

item.title: 古巴因燃料供应短缺遭遇全国性大停电
item.description: 周一，古巴电网发生全面停电，这个岛国正因美国为向共产主义政府施压而实施的实质性石油封锁，面临燃料供应短缺的困境。【原文：Cuba’s electric grid suffered a total power failure Monday as the island nation struggles with fuel supplies amid a de facto US oil blockade to pressure the communist government.】

item.title: 芝加哥地区190亿美元的税单引发了评估员职位的争夺战
item.description: 芝加哥房地产行业因一项价值190亿美元的税单而爆发激烈争论，业主们认为该税单阻碍了新建项目，并导致租金飙升。【原文：Chicago’s real estate industry has ignited a battle over a $19 billion tax bill that property owners say is holding back new construction and contributing to surging rents.】

item.title: 奥斯卡获奖制片公司的剖析：票房火爆，利润微薄
item.description: Neon已连续三年斩获奥斯卡奖，但其主要收入来源是恐怖片和流媒体业务【原文：Neon has won Oscars three years in a row, but it makes more money from horror films and streaming】

item.title: 就连主要能源出口国澳大利亚也难以幸免于伊朗战争的影响
item.description: 随着中东战争进入第三周，澳大利亚——国内炼油能力所剩无几——正表明，即便是主要的化石燃料出口国也无法幸免于日益加剧的能源危机。【原文：As the war in the Middle East enters its third week, Australia — with not much domestic refining left — is showcasing how even major fossil fuel exporters aren’t immune to the widening energy crunch.】

item.title: 莫斯科争分夺秒，趁油价飙升、制裁暂停之际大赚一笔
item.description:
随着原油从俄罗斯的太平洋和北极港口大量涌出，出货量创下一年多来最大增幅【原文：

Shipments surged by the most in more than a year as crude flooded out of Russia’s Pacific and Arctic ports

】

item.title: 沃伯格计划以25亿美元以上的价格出售汽车贷款机构埃克塞特
item.description: 据知情人士透露，华平投资正处于考虑出售次级汽车贷款机构埃克塞特金融（Exeter Finance）的初期阶段，此项交易预计可筹得25亿至30亿美元。【原文：Warburg Pincus is in the early stages of weighing a sale of subprime auto lender Exeter Finance that could fetch $2.5 billion to $3 billion, according to people familiar with the matter.】

item.title: Sonos首席执行官致力于耳机业务，但不涉足车载音响领域
item.description: Sonos公司首席执行官汤姆·康拉德表示，尽管该公司在该领域的首次尝试未能赢得消费者青睐，但公司仍致力于耳机产品线。【原文：Sonos Inc. Chief Executive Officer Tom Conrad said the company remains committed to the headphones category, even though its first effort in that space failed to win over consumers.】
